title: Aircall Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Aircall** is a cloud-based business telephony platform exposing a Public REST API at `https://api.aircall.io/v1`. This connector — `AircallConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "aircall"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from an Aircall account:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Lifecycle | `/ping` | Credential health probe |
| Calls | `/calls` | List + read call events; transfer / assign / start outbound |
| Users | `/users` | List + read agents |
| Numbers | `/numbers` | List + read DIDs |
| Contacts | `/contacts` | CRUD on CRM contacts |
| Tags | `/tags` | List call tags |
| Teams | `/teams` | List teams of agents |
| Webhooks | `/webhooks` | List + create webhook subscriptions |

The connector normalises calls into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{connector_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::AircallHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate Aircall API credentials

1. Sign in to https://dashboard.aircall.io.
2. Open **Integrations & API → Aircall API** (admin only).
3. Click **Generate an API Key**, give it a descriptive name (e.g. *Shielva ACP*), and select the scopes you need (Calls, Users, Numbers, Contacts, Tags, Teams, Webhooks).
4. Copy the **API ID** and **API Token** — Aircall shows the token only once.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Aircall**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `api_id` (text) | API ID from step 4 |
| `api_token` (secret) | API Token from step 4 |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://api.aircall.io/v1` (rarely needed) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 60 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `api_id` + `api_token` are present, probes `GET /ping`, and on success persists the credentials and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, CONNECTED)`. On 401 it returns `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which hits `GET /ping` as a lightweight probe. The result is mapped via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
200 → ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY,   CONNECTED)
401 → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)   — logged as warning
network → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Aircall Public API uses **HTTP Basic authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry.

### Credential model

- `api_id` is the Basic-auth **username**.
- `api_token` is the Basic-auth **password**.
- Wire format: `Authorization: Basic base64(api_id:api_token)`.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `api_id` + `api_token`. Probes `GET /ping`. On success, persists config. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a stable `TokenInfo` (access_token = api_token, token_type = `"Basic"`) — there is no token exchange. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /ping` — minimal probe; maps status via `_STATUS_MAP`. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None at the SDK layer; credentials live in `self.config`. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
Authorization: Basic base64(api_id:api_token)
Content-Type:  application/json
Accept:        application/json
```

The Basic header is computed lazily in `AircallHTTPClient._basic_auth_header()` so rotating `api_id`/`api_token` via `set_credentials()` takes effect on the next request without re-creating the client.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `api_id` | string | yes | `self.api_id` | Basic-auth username |
| `api_token` | secret | yes | `self.api_token` | Basic-auth password |
| `base_url` | string | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://api.aircall.io/v1`) | Sandbox / proxy override |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 60) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["api_id", "api_token"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_AIRCALL_BASE = "https://api.aircall.io/v1"
```

### Retry tuning (`client/http_client.py` module constants)

```python
_RETRY_BASE_DELAY_S = 1.0
_RETRY_BACKOFF      = 2.0
_RETRY_MAX_DELAY_S  = 32.0
_RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS = 3
```

Change these in one place; nothing else in the codebase should hardcode retry counts.

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `AircallConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::AircallHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `api_id` + `api_token`; probe `GET /ping`; persist config.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth — returns a stable TokenInfo with `token_type="Basic"`.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
`GET /ping`. Maps failures by `_STATUS_MAP`: 401 → OFFLINE/TOKEN_EXPIRED, 403 → DEGRADED/INVALID_CREDENTIALS, 429 → DEGRADED/CONNECTED, network → OFFLINE/CONNECTED.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Pages `GET /calls`, normalises each call → `NormalizedDocument` → `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult(documents_found/synced/failed, status)`. In incremental mode (`full=False`) it caps at 5 pages.

children.title: Calls
children.content:
### `async list_calls(per_page=50, page=1, from_date=None, to_date=None, direction=None, user_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /calls`. `from_date` / `to_date` are Unix epoch seconds (string). `direction` must be `"inbound"` or `"outbound"` (ValueError otherwise).

### `async get_call(call_id) -> Dict`
`GET /calls/{call_id}`.

### `async start_outbound_call(user_id, number_id, to) -> Dict`
`POST /users/{user_id}/calls` with body `{number_id, to}`. `to` is validated by `helpers.utils.is_valid_phone` (loose E.164).

### `async transfer_call(call_id, user_id) -> Dict`
`POST /calls/{call_id}/transfers` with body `{user_id}`.

### `async assign_call(call_id, user_id) -> Dict`
`PUT /calls/{call_id}/assignment` with body `{user_id}`.

children.title: Users + Numbers
children.content:
### `async list_users(per_page=50, page=1) -> Dict`
`GET /users?per_page={N}&page={P}`.

### `async get_user(user_id) -> Dict`
`GET /users/{user_id}`.

### `async list_numbers(per_page=50, page=1) -> Dict`
`GET /numbers?per_page={N}&page={P}`.

### `async get_number(number_id) -> Dict`
`GET /numbers/{number_id}`.

children.title: Contacts
children.content:
### `async list_contacts(per_page=50, page=1, search=None) -> Dict`
`GET /contacts`. `search` filter is forwarded as a query param.

### `async get_contact(contact_id) -> Dict`
`GET /contacts/{contact_id}`.

### `async create_contact(first_name, last_name, company_name=None, phone_numbers=None, emails=None) -> Dict`
`POST /contacts`. At least one of `first_name` / `last_name` is required (ValueError otherwise).

### `async update_contact(contact_id, first_name=None, last_name=None, company_name=None, phone_numbers=None, emails=None) -> Dict`
`POST /contacts/{contact_id}` with a partial payload. At least one field is required.

### `async delete_contact(contact_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /contacts/{contact_id}`. Returns `{}` on 204.

children.title: Tags · Teams · Webhooks
children.content:
### `async list_tags() -> Dict`
`GET /tags`.

### `async list_teams(per_page=50) -> Dict`
`GET /teams?per_page={N}`.

### `async list_webhooks(per_page=50, page=1) -> Dict`
`GET /webhooks?per_page={N}&page={P}`.

### `async create_webhook(url, events=None) -> Dict`
`POST /webhooks` with body `{url, events?}`. `url` must be non-empty (ValueError otherwise). `events` is the list of Aircall event types (e.g. `call.created`, `call.ended`, `contact.created`).

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
AircallError                          # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── AircallAuthError                  # 401 / 403
├── AircallBadRequestError            # 400
├── AircallNotFoundError              # 404
├── AircallConflictError              # 409
├── AircallRateLimitError             # 429 — retry_after_s
├── AircallServerError                # 5xx
└── AircallNetworkError               # transport (timeout / DNS / reset)
```

Back-compat alias preserved:
```
AircallNotFound = AircallNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately (no retry) |
| 429 | Exponential backoff `_RETRY_BASE_DELAY_S * 2 ** attempt + jitter`, retry up to `_RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS=3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` / `httpx.ConnectError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `AircallNetworkError` |

The orchestration layer (`connector.py`) additionally wraps each public read in `helpers.utils.with_retry(max_retries=3)` so that unexpected transient errors that escape the HTTP client (e.g. JSON-decode flakes on a flaky proxy) get one more chance to converge.

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)   — logged as warning
network → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, CONNECTED)
other → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `api_id` or `api_token` blank | Re-enter both in ACP. Aircall shows the token only once at generation — rotate if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at install | 401 from `/ping` — wrong api_id / api_token pair | Verify the pair in Aircall Dashboard → Integrations & API. Regenerate the key pair and re-install. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 — API token lacks the scope for a method | In Aircall Dashboard → Integrations & API, edit the key and grant the relevant scopes (Calls / Users / Numbers / Contacts / Tags / Teams / Webhooks). |
| `aircall.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Aircall per-token rate cap of ~60/min) | The client retries with exponential backoff. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer requests. |
| 404 on `get_call` / `get_contact` / `get_number` | Wrong ID, or the resource lives in a different Aircall account | Cross-check the ID in the Aircall Dashboard. IDs are integers, not UUIDs. |
| `ValueError: 'to' is not a valid phone number` | `start_outbound_call(..., to=...)` received a malformed number | Use E.164 (`+1XXXXXXXXXX`) or a recognised local format. Aircall rejects bare digits without country code. |
| `ValueError: direction must be 'inbound' or 'outbound'` | `list_calls(direction=...)` received an unsupported value | Only `"inbound"` and `"outbound"` are valid. |
| `AircallConflictError` on `create_contact` | Email or phone collides with an existing contact | Use `list_contacts(search=...)` to find the duplicate, then `update_contact` instead. |
| Webhook deliveries 401 at your endpoint | Aircall signs payloads with a per-account signing secret | Verify the signature using the secret from Aircall Dashboard → Integrations & API → Webhooks (handled outside this connector). |
